name: Amazon Seller MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server wrapping Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) — Amazon's official API for third-party sellers. Enables AI agents to manage product listings, inventory, orders, pricing, and seller analytics for Amazon marketplace sellers through the SP-API.
use_cases: Managing Amazon product listings and inventory from e-commerce automation agents
Monitoring and responding to orders from order management agents
Analyzing seller performance metrics and advertising data from analytics agents
Automating repricing and inventory restocking from marketplace management agents
not_for: Consumers without an Amazon Seller account
Read-only product research without seller account credentials
best_when: An Amazon seller or e-commerce agency wants AI agents to automate seller operations — listing management, inventory, order processing, and analytics — through Amazon's official SP-API.
avoid_when: You're not an Amazon seller. SP-API requires an active Amazon Seller account with API access enabled through Seller Central.

auth.notes: Amazon SP-API OAuth 2.0 with LWA (Login with Amazon) credentials. Requires AWS IAM role, SP-API developer registration, and seller account authorization. Complex multi-step setup.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SP-API is free to access; requires Amazon Seller account
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API access; seller account fees apply
pricing.notes: SP-API access is free. Amazon Seller account has monthly fees ($39.99 Professional plan). MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: SP-API rate limits vary by operation; throttling common for high-volume queries

known_agent_gotchas: SP-API auth is COMPLEX: requires AWS IAM role + LWA credentials + seller authorization — significant setup
SP-API throttling is aggressive — implement exponential backoff for all calls
Order and inventory modifications have real financial consequences — test extensively before autonomous use
API requires separate approval per SP-API role/scope — review which capabilities you need
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS to Amazon SP-API. Multi-layer authentication (LWA + AWS IAM). Seller data and orders are commercially sensitive. Rate limiting enforced. Pricing changes and inventory adjustments have direct financial impact.

security_score_components.security_notes: Amazon SP-API with LWA + AWS IAM auth. Seller operations have financial impact. SOC2 compliant. Complex but secure auth chain.
